UPDATES PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 SE MARTINSBURG. THIS WAS DETERMINED TO BE A SEPARATE TORNADO FROM THE ONE THAT DEVELOPED SOUTHWEST OF KEOTA WHICH BECAME AN EF4. PEAK WIND 150 MPH AND MAX WIDTH 1000 YARDS.

LARGE TORNADO ON THE GROUND.

UPDATES PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 4 W TALLEYRAND. THIS WAS THE TORNADO THAT DEVELOPED IN EASTERN KEOKUK COUNTY SOUTHWEST OF KEOTA THAT WENT THROUGH NORTHWESTERN WASHINGTON COUNTY AND EVENTUALLY DISSIPATED IN FAR SOUTHWESTERN JOHNSON COUNTY. THIS TORNADO WAS AN EF4 WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 170 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 700 YARDS. THIS TORNADO SEVERELY DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES NEAR KEOTA. A CAR WAS LOFTED IN THE AIR AND TOSSED ABOUT 1000 FEET INTO A NEARBY FIELD AND TREES WERE COMPLETELY DEBARKED WITH ONLY STUBS OF THE LARGEST BRANCHES REMAINING. THE TORNADO KNOCKED OVER A 325 FT CELL PHONE TOWER WEST OF WELLMAN.
MICHAEL HARTLEY HEADING TOWARDS KALONA SPOTTER REPORT.
CYLE DICKENS NWS CHAT.

SATELLITE AND RADAR DATA SUGGEST THAT A DIFFERENT TORNADO FORMED. DAT POINTS FROM AREA SUGGEST EF0 WITH 85 MPH WINDS ALONG ITS 6 MILE PATH. WIDTH IS UNKNOWN. THE STORM CYCLED AGAIN AND THEN PRODUCED THE CORALVILLE EF2.
CONFIRMED TORNADO ON THE GROUND BEING FOLLOWED BY SPOTTERS.
DEPUTY WATCHING, REPORTED IN CHAT ROOM.
EF2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 115 MPH. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED NORTHWEST OF VINTON, IOWA AND MOVED NORTHEAST, PRODUCING DAMAGE TO FARM BUILDINGS, TREES, AND POWER POLES.

*** 2 INJ *** A TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST DESTROYING TREES, A FEW OUTBUILDINGS, AND A GRAIN BIN IN RURAL JOHNSON COUNTY. THEN THE TORNADO CROSSED HWY 218 HEAVILY DAMAGING THE JAMES ST WAREHOUSE NEAR HWY 6 IN CORALVILLE. SEVERAL TRAILERS AND A TRUCK WERE FLIPPED OVER IN VICINITY. A RETAIL BUILDING ALSO HAD PART OF A ROOF LIFTED OFF WITH SEVERAL VEHICLES ROLLED OR FLIPPED. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED INTO A RESIDENTIAL AREA DESTROYING MANY TREES THAT BECAME UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. AT LEAST 2 INJURIES WERE REPORTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AT THIS TIME. THE TORNADO THEN DISSIPATING BEFORE REACHING INTERSTATE 80.
FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED 70 MPH WITH DAMAGE TO HOMES. REPORT RELAYED BY THE EMERGENCY MANAGER.
BARNS DOWN NEAR VINTON AFTER INITIAL LINE WENT THROUGH, POSSIBLE TORNADO.
ESTIMATED NEAR 70 MPH WINDS. LARGE BARN WAS DAMAGED, TRAILER DAMAGED, TREE BRANCHES DOWN, AND AN OUTBUILDING WITH DAMAGE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 E CHENEY TO CHANGE FROM A PUBLIC REPORT TO AN NWS STORM SURVEY. SATELLITE DERIVED PATH OF A TORNADO HERE. NO DAMAGE SEEN BUT PATH IN FIELD SEEN. THERE WAS NO DAMAGE WITH WHICH TO RATE THIS TORNADO. TORNADO NORTHWEST OF URBANA WAS THE GROUND FOR 1.35 MILES.
SATELLITE DERIVED PATH OF A TORNADO HERE. NO DAMAGE SEEN BUT PATH IN FIELD SEEN. THERE WAS NO DAMAGE WITH WHICH TO RATE THIS TORNADO. TORNADO NORTHWEST OF URBANA WAS THE GROUND FOR 1.35 MILES.

EF2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 115 MPH. A TORNADO PRODUCED A SHORT PATH OF SIGNIFICANT TREE AND FARM BUILDING DAMAGE, AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO A HOME. IT LIFTED AS IT APPROACHED BEARBOWER SAND PRAIRIE ON THE BUCHANAN COUNTY LINE.

A TORNADO BEGAN SOUTHWEST OF HILLS MOVING NORTHEAST CROSSING HWY 218 THROUGH THE NORTHWEST PORTIONS OF TOWN, DESTROYING SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS, LARGE TREES, AND TRIM/SIDING ON HOUSES. THE GREATEST DAMAGE WAS SEEN IN WESTERN PORTIONS OF THE TOWN OF HILLS WHERE ROOFS WERE COMPLETELY TORN OFF, SIDING REMOVED FROM SEVERAL HOMES AND A LARGE OUTBUILDING DESTROYED. THE TORNADO THEN PROCEEDED THROUGH A WOODED AREA WITH SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED. IT THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST DESTROYING SIDING AND ROOFS ON SEVERAL HOMES ON THE EXTREME SOUTHEAST SIDE OF IOWA CITY BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST PAST THE RAILROAD TRACKS NORTHEAST OF HWY 6.
HAIL INCREASING AND SHEET METAL AND INSULATION FALLING FROM THE SKY.
SATELLITE DERIVED TRACK. NO DAMAGE NOTED, TIME BASED ON RADAR. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.66 MILES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 104 MPH TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF WYOMING AND MOVED THROUGH THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN DAMAGING MAINLY TREES WITH AT LEAST TWO TREES SNAPPED OFF. TWISTED METAL WAS LOFTED INTO THE FIRE DEPARTMENT PARKING LOT.

SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS FELL ON MOBILE HOMES DAMAGING ROOFS AND SIDING. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED QUICKLY ON THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN. PEAK WIND 120 MPH. MAX WIDTH 150 YARDS. AND LENGTH 1 MILE.

BRIEF TORNADO. RIPPED OFF THE ENTIRE ROOF OF A GAS STATION AND TOSSED IT INTO A BRICK BUILDING TO THE SOUTH. THE FRONT WALL OF THIS BUILDING COLLAPSED. THE TORNADO ALSO UPROOTED TREES.

A LOCALIZED RFD SURGE ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF A BROADER HP SUPERCELL RFD CAUSED A TORNADO TO FORM. THE EF2 TORNADO WITH 115 MPH WINDS BEGUN JUST EAST OF GENESEO AT 10:15 AM. THE PATH HAD SPORADIC DAMAGE AND BASED ON EYE-WITNESS REPORTS IT WAS A MULTIPLE VORTEX TORNADO. DIFFERENT SUCTION VORTICES CAUSED DAMAGE TO FARM BUILDINGS, TREES, SILOS AND HOUSES ALONG ITS 2.8 MILE LONG PATH. MANY TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED IN DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS. MOST HOUSES HAD UPLIFT OF SHINGLES. TOWARDS THE END OF THE PATH, THE TORNADO STRENGTHENED JUST BEFORE IT OCCLUDED. EF2 DAMAGE WAS FOUND HERE AS A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED, 4 FARM OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND THE ROOF ON THE HOUSE WAS LIFTED THEN PUT BACK DOWN ON THE HOUSE. MANY THANKS TO HENRY COUNTY OEM FOR HELPING WITH THE SURVEY.

EF-1 RATED TORNADO WITH PEAK WIND GUST OF 105 MPH. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF INDUSTRY, IL NEAR HWY 67 DAMAGING BARNS, OUTBUILDINGS, AND SNAPPING MANY LARGE HARDWOOD TREES. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED TO TRACK TO THE NORTHEAST CAUSING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL EMPTY GRAIN BINS AND DESTROYING A PORTION OF A ROOF OF A HOUSE. OCCASIONAL DAMAGE WAS FOUND TO TREES, OUTBUILDINGS, AND GRAIN BINS ACROSS FAR EAST-CENTRAL MCDONOUGH COUNTY AS THE TORNADO APPEARED TO BE DISSIPATING JUST BEFORE THE MCDONOUGH-FULTON COUNTY LINE.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 E MALVERN. A SPORADIC EF1 TORNADO BEGUN JUST EAST OF MORRISON IL AROUND 3:56 PM. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A POWER POLE AND A TREE AT THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO THEN CAUSED SPORADIC DAMAGE UNTIL IT APPROACHED FULFS ROAD. AT THIS LOCATION IT STRENGTHENED AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO TREES, FARM OUTBUILDINGS AND A POWER POLE. THE TORNADO SHORTLY ENDED AFTER THIS LOCATION. WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED IN THE 105-110 MPH RANGE. AT ITS WIDEST, THE TORNADO WAS 300 YARDS AND WAS LIKELY A MULTI-VORTEX TORNADO.

EF-1 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS STORM SURVEY IN TANDEM WITH MUSCATINE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT. THIS TORNADO DEVELOPED JUST NORTH-NORTHWEST OF WEST LIBERTY AND TRACKED ACROSS THE FAR NORTHEAST PART OF TOWN BEFORE EVENTUALLY CROSSING OVER U.S. HIGHWAY 6 BETWEEN WEST LIBERTY AND ATALISSA AND DIMINISHING SHORTLY AFTER. THE EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE WAS MAINLY TO OUTBUILDINGS, WITH SEVERAL WALLS AND ROOFS OF THESE COLLAPSED AND IN SOME CASES BLOWN ACROSS A DOWNSTREAM FIELD. A FEW LARGE TREES WERE ALSO SNAPPED IN ADDITION TO TWO UTILITY POLES. A LARGE METAL BATTING CAGE ANCHORED INTO CONCRETE WAS TWISTED AND BENT SHARPLY AT ITS BASE. TORNADO LASTED 6 MINUTES ON THE GROUND BEFORE LIFTING.

A tornado developed about 2 miles east of Walcott along Interstate 80. The tornado tracked east and caused sporadic minor tree and structure damage at the Northwest Boulevard exit just north of I-80. The tornado then moved across the Davenport Airport, and caused damage to canopies, tents, and equipment. The tornado passed just south of the NWS office, then continued east, producing minor tree and structure damage along 210th St. The tornado followed an intermittent path of around 9.6 miles, and was on the ground for about 14 minutes. The tornado was rated EF-0, with maximum estimated winds around 75 mph. Occasional minor tree damage was also observed south of the path, due to straight line winds.

A brief tornado developed in Clinton Friday evening, and crossed the Mississippi River before dissipating near Fulton. The tornado caused sporadic tree damage, including several large trees on the courthouse lawn. The tornado was captured on webcam video at the riverfront, where it moved a boat docked near the Candlelight Inn. Maximum winds were estimated around 75 mph. The tornado was rated EF-0.

Video evidence of multiple brief touchdowns of suction vortices. Chaser video shows three distinct tornado locations along this path. No reports of damage of been received. Very sporadic touchdowns along this 8 mile path were reported.

Video and photo evidence indicate a brief tornado in open farm ground. The tornado is rated EF-U as there is no observable damage other than a convergent path in the corn. The tornado was on the ground for 1.23 miles with a path width of 20 yards.
